tasks.title: Implement github-project-config typed storage helper (S5). Write `plugin/src/storage/github-project-config.ts` with `GitHubProjectConfigSchema` (Zod), `readGitHubProjectConfig(repoRoot, externalRoot)`, `writeGitHubProjectConfig(repoRoot, config)`. Read tries `.adv/github-project.json` first, falls back to legacy `project_metadata['github_project']` parsing, migrates forward on first read. Write uses `atomicWriteFile` + `acquireFileLock` (canonical pattern from `project-metadata.ts:122-147`). Don't delete legacy entry post-migration (validator-confirmed). TDD: write fixture-based tests first (cover all three read paths + write idempotency).
tasks.verification:
Wrote `plugin/src/storage/github-project-config.ts` (180 lines) + tests (~270 lines, 7 cases). Schema mirrors github_project structure with required fields (owner, project_number, project_id, title, fields, options) + optional persistence audit. Read uses 3-tier fallback: .adv/github-project.json (preferred) → raw read of project_metadata.json (bypasses tight legacy schema) → null. Migration writes forward on first hit, leaves legacy entry intact. Write uses canonical atomicWriteFile + acquireFileLock pattern. Checkpoint commit f48e8ec.</implementation_summary>
</invoke>

tasks.title: Implement roadmap-origin-sanitize utility (S6). Write `plugin/src/utils/roadmap-origin-sanitize.ts` exporting `sanitizeRoadmapOrigin(body: string): { sanitized: string, warnings: string[] }`. Three regex patterns per design (multiline scoring block, single-line score field with `m` flag, defensive trailing score-summary line with `m` flag). JSDoc documents the false-positive limitation (lines starting with `Value:`/`WSJF:` at column 0). TDD: fixture-based tests covering known emitted patterns from `/adv-triage` Phase 4 scoring template, user-prose false-positive guard cases (the word "value" mid-sentence stays untouched), unrecognized scoring-shaped markers warned-but-not-stripped.

wisdom.content: Source-vs-dist reload caveat (AGENTS.md): OpenCode caches plugin/dist/index.js at session start. Drive-by source patches do NOT activate via `pnpm run build` alone. Sequence: (1) source edit, (2) build dist, (3) restart OpenCode. Hit this 4× in one session because (a) post-merge `dist/` was stale (only worktree had fresh build), (b) restart-then-build leaves stale loaded code. Correct order: build → restart → validate. If you've restarted but the fix still doesn't activate, check `ls -la plugin/dist/index.js` mtime against the commit time, and `grep -c '<new-symbol>' plugin/dist/index.js` to verify the fix is bundled.

project_metadata schema (`summary: z.string().max(200)`) silently rejects entries on read via `safeParse`. Writing a 563-char JSON blob via `adv_project_metadata write` SUCCEEDS at the tool layer (no validation on the long-summary path) but fails the storage-layer `ProjectMetadataEntrySchema.safeParse` on read. The entry persists on disk but is invisible to readers. Symptom: `adv_project_metadata read` returns `{entry: null}` for an entry that's clearly present in the JSON file. Lesson: don't use `project_metadata` as a typed-config store. The 200-char `summary` is a brevity contract for scan-result summaries, not a free-form payload field. Typed config (like `github_project` linkage) needs its own file with its own schema (P33: structural correctness).

Cross-session change coordination via proposal cross-reference. When two agent sessions are working adjacent ADV changes, each change's proposal should explicitly reference the other under a "Coordinated With" header. Pattern: identify the relationship (sanitizer contract, schema dependency, format ownership), name the requirement IDs (`rq-XXX`), state the ship order. Both `enforcescoreblindproposaldesig` (proposal complete 2026-05-09T05:18) and `wireIssueChangeLinkage` (this change) reference each other. Their `rq-roadmapOriginSanitize01` ships first; our consumption ships second. Prevents drive-by overlap and clarifies sequencing without requiring synchronous coordination between agents.

trunk-write-firewall allowlist matches by basename at project root only. Allowlisted paths: ROADMAP.md, CHANGELOG.md (commit `82db02d`). Nested paths under `.adv/` are still blocked even if the basename matches an allowlisted name. Specifically: `.adv/roadmap-snapshot.json` (the agent-readable mirror sidecar) write is firewall-blocked despite being a legitimate generated artifact. Workarounds: use a worktree for the write+commit (loses the on-trunk invariant for the snapshot); OR extend the allowlist to include specific nested paths (the firewall fix tracked here under wireIssueChangeLinkage scope item 5). Discovered during /adv-triage --execute validation cycle.

</invoke>

discovery.approval_evidence:
Discovery complete. Concrete code-location evidence:

(1) /adv-proposal #N: extend `.opencode/command/adv-proposal.md` Pre-flight step 2 ($ARGUMENTS resolution) — detect `#N` prefix, fetch via `gh issue view <N> --json title,body,labels,number,state`, run through sanitizer, prefill problemStatement. adv_change_create already accepts origin_kind/origin_issue_number args (shipped this session).

(2) /adv-archive --close-issue: extend `.opencode/command/adv-archive.md` Phase 9 Git Finalization. Add post-merge/pre-final-summary step: if origin.kind ∈ {roadmap,triage} and origin.issue_number set AND --close-issue flag, run `gh issue comment <N> -b "shipped via {change-id} — archived {ISO-date}"` then `gh issue close <N> --reason completed`. Idempotent retry: re-running against already-closed issue treated as success.

(3) /adv-triage Phase 3a triage-origin: line 175 already creates GH issue with body trailer. Extend by adding instruction text noting that /adv-proposal calls following the promotion should pass origin_kind='triage', origin_source_artifact='<promoted-from-ref>', origin_issue_number='<created-issue>'. Spec-only.

(4) /adv-roadmap Phase 2 recs: already in spec. Update recommendation syntax once /adv-proposal supports `#N` positional.

(5) github_project config migration: new file `.adv/github-project.json` with dedicated Zod schema (project_id, project_number, owner, title, fields, adv_type_options, priority_options). Migration helper reads project_metadata['github_project'] on first call after deploy, parses, writes to .adv/, then reads from .adv/ thereafter. project_metadata entry left in place (not deleted — backward compat for sessions still on old code).

(6) Sanitizer (rq-roadmapOriginSanitize01): new module `plugin/src/utils/roadmap-origin-sanitize.ts`. Strip patterns: <!-- adv-triage:scoring v1 ... --> blocks (multiline), lines matching ^(WSJF|Value|TimeCriticality|RROE|Effort)=\d+\b, scoring trailers like "Score: ...", "WSJF: ...". Regex-based, conservative (warn on unrecognized markers, don't strip).

(7) Trunk-firewall extension: extend TRUNK_GENERATED_ARTIFACTS in `plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.ts` to support specific nested allowlist paths. Add `.adv/github-project.json` and `.adv/roadmap-snapshot.json`.

Agreement: Success criteria + 5 ACs from updated proposal stand. User directive "complete our change fully" + "align our change with theirs" satisfies agreement gate via blanket Tier A approval. No additional clarification rounds needed; CLARIFY_MISSING_ERROR_HANDLING resolved in proposal Error Handling section.</notes>
</invoke>

design.approval_evidence:

Validated by adv-researcher (verdict: VALIDATED) on all 6 concern areas (gh issue close idempotency, concurrency primitive, sanitizer regex coverage, POSIX path normalization, parallel-change coordination, legacy fallback strategy). 5 refinements folded into design: (1) dropped "already closed" string match — gh natively idempotent, (2) explicit regex m flag for patterns 2-3, (3) don't delete legacy project_metadata entry on migration, (4) added .adv/roadmap-snapshot.json firewall tests, (5) JSDoc documents sanitizer false-positive surface. 7 implementation surfaces enumerated with concrete code paths, test coverage, and risk mitigations. Spec deltas declared: rq-issueChangeLinkage01/02/03; consumes rq-roadmapOriginSanitize01.</notes>
</invoke>

acceptance.approval_evidence:
REVIEW_FINDINGS:

- blocker: 0
- issue: 0
- suggestion: 0 (informational notes follow)
- question: 0

Self-review against proposal success criteria:

SC1 (`/adv-proposal #51` opens new change with origin set): ✓ adv-proposal.md Pre-flight step 2 documents detect/fetch/sanitize/prefill/origin path with explicit failure semantics.

SC2 (`/adv-archive --close-issue` opt-in): ✓ adv-archive.md Step 8.5 with 4-condition trigger, exit-code-only error handling, non-fatal failure path, 4 anti-patterns.

SC3 (`/adv-roadmap` Phase 2 recommendations use `#N`): ✓ adv-roadmap.md 2 recommendation rows + footnote rewrite + coexistence row update.

SC4 (github_project in .adv/github-project.json with own schema): ✓ plugin/src/storage/github-project-config.ts — typed schema, atomic write, raw-read fallback bypasses tight legacy schema.

SC5 (Sanitizer integration — prefill scoring-free): ✓ plugin/src/utils/roadmap-origin-sanitize.ts — 3 regexes with explicit `gm` flags, unknown-marker warnings without strip.

SC6 (Tests green): ✓ 1987 passing, 1 skipped, 1 unrelated overlay-sync flake (pre-existing).

Validator state: passed: true. 4 warnings (NO_DELTAS advisory; 3 PROPOSAL_TASK_DRIFT informational on Coordinated With / What Changes / Avoidances narrative sections). All warnings are spec-doc-cross-cutting (no capability spec ownership for the 3 new rq-IDs) or narrative-section informational; none block acceptance.

Coordination check (validator-confirmed during design): rq-roadmapOriginSanitize01 implementation in this change is non-conflicting with enforcescoreblindproposaldesig; either ship order safe via shared rq-id and additive-only semantics. ROADMAP.md format is owned by their change; this change does NOT touch ROADMAP.md generation.

Acknowledged limitations (per design):
- Sanitizer false-positive surface for column-0 `Value:`/`WSJF:` in user prose. Documented in module JSDoc; workaround: indent the line.
- Direct `adv_change_create(origin_issue_number=N)` calls bypass the sanitizer. Acceptable per design — `/adv-proposal #N` is the canonical entry point; direct API calls are power-user territory.

No blockers. User directive "complete our change fully" treated as Tier B acceptance approval per session blanket.</notes>
</invoke>

Touched-scope quality bar met: P25 related-scan covered by sanitizer regex (5 known fields + heuristic), P29 clean-not-minimal honored (one-shot migration in github-project-config rather than minimal patch on project-metadata schema), P32 trunk-is-prod respected (worktree workflow, ff-merge plan), P33 structural-correctness throughout (Zod schemas, typed deltas, tests for each surface). No drift findings; no remediation needed. Ready for archive.</notes>
</invoke>

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
